articles.description: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar dissolved his cabinet on Tuesday and tendered his resignation to Governor Syed Ata Hasnain at Lok Bhavan in Patna. He took oath as a Member of the Rajya Sabha on April 10.

articles.title: Are young Indians under or overworked? Both are true but AI may change work hours as we go along
articles.description: The growth of India’s economy is a means, not an end in itself. It must translate into good jobs and better lives. However, Time Use survey data shows significant disparities in the hours that India’s young work. Could AI adoption reduce these gaps?

articles.title: Countdown to the mega IPO of SpaceX: A giant leap for insiders, a gigantic risk for public investors?
articles.description: SpaceX's IPO has more riding on it than size alone: indices like Nasdaq and S&amp;P Dow Jones seem eager to rewrite inclusion rules for mega listings. Are equity platforms tilting away from investor interest? Index providers must decide who they serve: investors who track them or issuers who game them.

articles.title: New study reveals self-cleaning mechanism of green pill millipede in Western Ghats
articles.description: Study demonstrates that the millipede’s exoskeleton possesses advanced hydrophobic properties

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why govt wants stricter gold hallmarking and what will change for buyers
articles.description: The government is mandating a digital trail for gold jewellery, requiring jewellers to link photographs and dimensions to every Hallmarked Unique ID. This overhaul aims to eliminate the duplication of certificates and ensure the purity promised matches the item in the buyer's hand.

articles.title: Flavour puzzle: physics doesn’t know why matter comes in ‘flavours’
articles.description: Addressing the flavour puzzle wouldn’t merely tidy up loose ends in an otherwise successful theory of forces and particles. It would reveal something profound about how nature organises its complexity at a fundamental level

articles.title: Parched again: On Bengaluru’s drinking water woes
articles.description: Bengaluru is treating water supply as infinitely expandable
